evidence_excerpt: Postnatally at 4 months, left Achilles tenotomy and right toe release were performed; at 18 months the infant was able to walk with normal lower extremity function.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Serial follow-up documented resolution of edema and normal movements of both feet; long-term report notes normal lower extremity function.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Five days after fetoscopic lysis, limb edema appeared reduced, distal blood flow was documented, and active movements were observed.
Postnatally at 4 months: left Achilles tenotomy and plastic surgery; right toe release.
At 18 months, the infant was able to walk with normal lower extremity function.

notes_on_uncertainty: Summary corresponds to Case 1 in the provided series. Index time approximated as the initial diagnosis at 18 weeks’ gestation. Long-term outcome inferred from the 18-month follow-up reporting normal lower extremity function.
